item.title: "I haven't heard that from anybody"
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell humiliates Donald Trump by fact-checking him live in front of cameras during a tour of the Fed's new renovations. This is how you put a pathological liar in his place.... "So we're taking a look, uh, and it looks like it's about $3.1 billion. It went up a little bit. Or a lot," said Trump, causing Powell to look at him in surprise and then shake his head. The president was visiting the Fed to inspect ongoing renovations to the central bank's buildings. Trump has been complaining about the cost of the work as he seeks to find justification for firing Powell. "Uh, so the 2.7 is now 3.1 and—" Trump continued. "I'm not aware of that," interrupted Powell. "Yeah, it just came out," insisted Trump, producing a document from his jacket pocket. "I haven't heard that from anybody at the Fed," said Powell. Republican Senator Tim Scott was standing beside Trump and agreed with his inflated number, proving once again that he's nothing but a spineless sycophant. "3.1," said Trump. "This came from us?" asked Powell, inspecting the document that Trump handed him. "Yes. I don't know who does that," said Trump. "You're including the Martin renovation. You just added in a third building is what that is. That's a third building," said Powell, visibly frustrated but maintaining his trademark patience. "Well, I know but it's a building that's being built," said Trump. "No, no, it was built five years ago. We finished Martin five tears ago," said Powell. "It's part of the overall work," insisted a clueless Trump. "It's not new," said Powell. Trump wants to terminate and replace Powell because he won't lower interest rates but Powell has repeatedly and patiently explained that the Fed can’t implement rate cuts yet because of the uncertainty injected into the world economy by the Trump tariffs.
item.dc:creator: LordoftheHildago

item.title: This just in
item.description: Front page edit: Well it seems like there's some movement for the files to be released. I just want these child touching ghouls to go the fuck away. I don't care who is on the list I want all of them to go away. I don't care if it does damage to the political parties, I don't care if lots of people get fired because of the nastiness that they do. They can be replaced we can move forward but we can't let them continue to permeate and be manipulating and molding the system to their will.

item.title: REPORT: Andry Hernandez returned to the US after being detained unlawfully and unconstitutionally in CECOT.
item.description:
Minor correction on the title of this post: He returned to Venezuela, not the US. Probably better that way anyhow. Hadn't seen anything about this, but spotted it and put a smile on my face. Glad he was returned home. Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/media-telecom/venezuelan-makeup-artist-returns-home-describes-torture-during-el-salvador-2025-07-23/
Now I want cake.
item.dc:creator: Mirrormancer

item.title: You don't know what box we've opened here
item.description: There is nothing to celebrate in the fact that payment processors are lobbying to control how we get to spend our money. That is what this all is and many of you don't care because it only affects the gaming industry right now but it's sent the message that they can get away with it and set the ground work for much worse.

item.dc:creator: coastalfoundhound

item.title: What costs $60,000 and won’t be getting used again?
item.description:
Weekly railroad shenanigans - though this one was a little worse than normal. This poor older gentleman got himself stuck about 45 seconds before a train would have come through on the clear track to the left. Fortunately, the engineer saw him from a curve 3/4 of a mile away and was only going ~45mph, slammed on the emergency brakes, and was able to come to a stop with about 200 feet to go. About 10 inches lateral clearance between life and death.
This is your friendly reminder to take away your parents’ keys when they get old. If they are unable to live by themselves, they should not be driving.
He made it 3/4 of a mile down the train tracks - half a mile of which was after his front driver side rim shattered from rubbing against one of the rails. The poor fellow had absolutely zero idea what was going on or where he was—the security footage shows him repeatedly putting it in park, stepping out of the cab, putting it in reverse trying to get out of there, and then eventually just flooring the gas pedal and seeing how far he could go in hopes he could get out.
The tow truck driver was kind enough to lend us his sling, which made the rescue afterwards relatively straightforward. Unfortunately, the man tore up a lot of the undercarriage, destroyed his front left wheel, lost a good chunk of his brake caliper, broke the axle, and severed a couple electrical lines, so it was going to have to be a heavy rescue, no matter what. Steering from inside the truck cab did no good.
Just… Be smart. Tracks are for trains, not cars.
item.dc:creator: kickback256

item.title: Well... I've had better days...
item.description: Had some shortness of breath the last couple days, decided it would be smart to go to the ER. Turns out I was in a hypertensive crisis. BP of 213/128. I made the right choice.

item.title: This has to stop. ICE hiring bounty hunters as if they were not bad enough already. Arresting people who have committed no crime. Deporting them without due process in defiance of the Supreme Court. $30k bounties. It’s fascism.
item.description:
Ok, this was a funny one. But think of this. This is enough for a brown skinned person to be pulled over, detained, and deported now.
This was before the BBB passed. They knew it was a foregone conclusion.
I took some of these screenshots myself from jobs that are real.
item.dc:creator: Wheretheheckisjason

item.title: This is it, folks. My Last Shitpost for Imgur.
item.description: It's been a wild ride, yall. I've been shitposting my way through this site across 3 presidents, a divorce, two catastrophic car accidents, a parent's death, 4 different jobs. Through all of it, this website (and the app) were an anchor of sorts, for me. Through all the highs and lows, trials and tribulation, laughter and tears (and a lot of physical pain in between,) this site and all of you guys helped push me through the absurdity with the upvotes, the comments, and the favorites that you scattered across the hundreds and hundreds of posts over the years. This decision is strictly selfish, and please understand that, as I mean it down to the core of my ginger soul. I have squandered my means of dopamine that came from every submission here. In short, the thrill isn't as thrilling as it used to be, anymore. I'm off to find another pasture to fertilize with my shitposts. I've no clue where I'll land, even if I land at all. Wherever it may be, though, rest assured that someone will likely be PMing me, asking for the source link for whatever the hell that I uploaded there. Yall have been awesome. I love you all. (Cept Dave. Fuck Dave.) I wish I could stay, but I've definitely worn out my welcome here. To @satan, I bequeath the entirety of the "Keanu Reeves Whoa" tag. What started off as a novelty tag turned into a 1,000 post collection of a lot of nonsense that made FP over the years. To @ElbowDeepInAHorse, I bequeath the entirety of my "100 percent authentic shitpost" tags. We never talked often, you and I. But you are, and always, shall be a legend here. I'd always looked up to you for inspiration on how unabashed your humor was delivered. To @deafkitten, I would offer you my dump collections, but yours have always made mine look miniscule in comparison, and I looked forward to every collection of yours every morning. Anyway, this is getting wordy and semi preachy. 7m upvotes, almost 300 trophies, and nearly a billion views. It was a good run. Keep it awesome. Keep it funny. Keep it Weird. @Maymyenemieslivelong, signing off the Net.
item.dc:creator: MayMyEnemiesLiveLong

item.title: Well there’s where all the Colbert money went…
item.description: $1.5 billion is insane money for South Park… If money actually was the issue for Colbert.. looks like they could have made better decisions. (South Park is on Paramount+. Colbert is on CBS. Paramount owns CBS)
